scope: wave2 fan-out migration: 3 of legacy's 5 read streams (users, calls, sms) are ported at Tier 1. contacts and phone_numbers keep their legacy Go implementation for now (see docs.md's Known limits — a validator/dialect coverage-bookkeeping gap, not a read-behavior gap); any other JustCall surface (SMS send, call actions, webhooks) is out of scope until Pass B.

excluded.reason: legacy reads this via POST with an empty-object body; api_surface.json's capabilities.write fail-first-run rule treats any non-excluded POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE endpoint as implying reverse-ETL write support, which this read-only connector must not falsely claim (internal/app/app.go gates real reverse-ETL execution on capabilities.write). Kept on the legacy Go implementation pending a dialect/tooling extension (e.g. a covered_by.read_via_post marker) that can represent a POST-shaped read without implying write capability; see docs.md
same POST-read-vs-write-capability tooling conflict as /v1/contacts/list above; kept on the legacy Go implementation; see docs.md
Pass B capability expansion; legacy connector is read-only
Pass B capability expansion; legacy connector is read-only
